zh.lifeSummary: 當John Atta del La Hurste於1302年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾時，他的父親Robert de la Hurste31歲，母親Mrs de la Hurste28歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Atta del La Hurste于1302年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾时，他的父亲Robert de la Hurste31岁，母亲Mrs de la Hurste28岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: del
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Lorraine): variant of the habitational name Delle 2 or a masculine form of a homonymous topographic name. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Spanish or Italian preposition with fused definite article del ‘of the’, which forms first part of surnames such as Del Cid and Del Signore , has apparently been in some instances counted as an independent surname.

variants.name: Delle
meanings: German: habitational name from a place so named in North Rhine-Westphalia or a topographic name (see   Dell  ).

French (Lorraine): habitational name from Delle, a place in Territoire de Belfort. Alternatively, a topographic name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone who lived near a prominent alder tree or trees, from elle, a derivative of Middle High German erle ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Hurste
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a wood or wooded hill, from Middle English hirst(e), herst(e), hurst(e) (Old English hyrst) or a habitational name from any of the places so called, such as Hurst Green (in Mitton, Lancashire), Hirst (Northumberland), Hurst (Berkshire, Kent, Warwickshire), Hurstpierpoint (Sussex), or Hirst in Longwood (Yorkshire).
Irish: re-Anglicized form of de Horsaigh, the Gaelicized form of the English habitational name Horsey , established in Ireland since the 13th century.
German and Swiss German (also Hürst): topographic name from Middle High German hurst ‘woodland, thicket’; or a habitational name from a place so named in Westphalia.

variants.name: Herst
meanings: English: variant of   Hurst  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from Polish herszt ‘ringleader, chieftain’.

variants.name: Horsey
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Horsey Pignes (Somerset), Horse Eye (Sussex), or Horsey (Norfolk), from Old English hors ‘horse’ + ēg ‘island, low-lying land’.

variants.name: Hearst
meanings: English: variant of   Hurst  .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname such as   Hirsch  .

variants.name: Horst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Phares, Alois, Armin, Fritz, Hans, Inge, Manfred, Monika, Reinhold.

North German and Dutch: topographic name from Middle Low German hurst, Middle Dutch horst ‘undergrowth, brushwood, wild place’. More specifically, the term was also used to denote a crow's or similar large bird's nest, a raised area in surrounding marshland, or an area of uncleared woodland, all of which meanings could have contributed to the surname. This surname is also a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. Compare Hurst .

History: As a name of German origin (see 1 above) the surname Horst is established among the American Mennonites. Their progenitors were the brothers Joseph, Michael, and Peter Horst, who came to PA in 1731 together with their widowed mother. Among their descendants the altered form Hurst is also found.

variants.name: Hirst
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hurst  .
German: variant of Hürst (see Hurst ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Atta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old Norse personal name Ásta, from ást ‘love‘.

variants.name: Astrid
meanings: name, derived from Old Norse áss ‘god’ + fríðr ‘fair, beautiful’. It came into use in the English-speaking world during the 20th century, influenced to some extent by Queen Astrid of the Belgians ( 1905–35 ).

en.lifeSummary: When John Atta del La Hurste was born in 1302, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de la Hurste, was 31 and his mother, Mrs de la Hurste, was 28. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Atta del La Hurste est né en 1302, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de la Hurste, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Mrs de la Hurste, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Atta del La Hurste 1302 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de la Hurste 31 und seine Mutter Mrs de la Hurste 28. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Atta del La Hurste è nato il nel 1302 nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert de la Hurste, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Mrs de la Hurste, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ko.lifeSummary: John Atta del La Hurste(이)가 1302년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de la Hurste(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs de la Hurste(은)는 28세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Atta del La Hurste nasceu em 1302, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert de la Hurste, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Mrs de la Hurste, tinha 28 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Atta del La Hurste родился в 1302 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert de la Hurste было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs de la Hurste, 28. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Atta del La Hurste nació en 1302, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert de la Hurste, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Mrs de la Hurste, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
